tweets.title: China has successfully landed a reusable rocket for the first time in a major breakthrough for the country's space programme, according to state media. The China Aerospace Science and Technology Corporation said the Long March 10B rocket lifted off from Hainan in southern China at 12:15 local time (9:45 AM IST) on Friday. Around six minutes after separating from the rocket's upper stage, its booster returned to Earth vertically and was recovered on a floating platform.
tweets.content:
China has successfully landed a reusable rocket for the first time in a major breakthrough for the country's space programme, according to state media.

The China Aerospace Science and Technology Corporation said the Long March 10B rocket lifted off from Hainan in southern China at 12:15 local time (9:45 AM IST) on Friday.

Around six minutes after separating from the rocket's upper stage, its booster returned to Earth vertically and was recovered on a floating platform.

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: The story of Baba Harbhajan Singh represents more than just a single soldier’s sacrifice. It reflects the deep bond between Indian soldiers, their regimental traditions, and the enduring respect shown to those who give their lives in service to the nation. More than five decades after his sacrifice, Baba Harbhajan Singh remains one of the Indian Army’s most revered figures. Whether seen as a symbol of unwavering duty, military tradition, or enduring remembrance, his legacy continues to inspire all who serve on India’s frontiers. 9/9

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: Born on August 30, 1946, in Punjab, Harbhajan Singh enlisted in the Punjab Regiment as a young sepoy. On October 4, 1968, while escorting a mule convoy carrying supplies near Nathu La, he slipped into a glacial stream and lost his life in the line of duty at just 22 years old. 3/9
tweets.content:
Born on August 30, 1946, in Punjab, Harbhajan Singh enlisted in the Punjab Regiment as a young sepoy. On October 4, 1968, while escorting a mule convoy carrying supplies near Nathu La, he slipped into a glacial stream and lost his life in the line of duty at just 22 years old.

3/9
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: The Nathu La sector is one of the most strategically important stretches of the India–China border. The agreement: - Enables uranium exports exclusively for peaceful purposes under IAEA safeguards - Supports India's goal of achieving 100 GW of nuclear power capacity by 2047 - Strengthens the India–Australia strategic partnership in energy, defence, and critical minerals Australia holds around 28% of the world's known uranium reserves, making the deal a significant step in securing fuel for India's expanding nuclear energy sector.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: India & Australia Sign Uranium Export Deal

India and Australia have signed a long-term agreement for the export of Australian uranium to support India's civilian nuclear energy programme.

The agreement:
- Enables uranium exports exclusively for peaceful purposes under IAEA safeguards
- Supports India's goal of achieving 100 GW of nuclear power capacity by 2047
- Strengthens the India–Australia strategic partnership in energy, defence, and critical minerals

Australia holds around 28% of the world's known uranium reserves, making the deal a significant step in securing fuel for India's expanding nuclear energy sector.

* ਇਹ ਸੀ 90 ਦਾ ਦਹਾਕਾ—ਖੂਨ ਨਾਲ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਇਤਿਹਾਸ। ਚੱਲੀ ਹੋਈ ਗੋਲੀ ਨੇ ਕਦੇ ਵੀ ਹਿੰਦੂ ਜਾਂ ਸਿੱਖ ਨਹੀਂ ਦੇਖਿਆ।

* ਹਥਿਆਰਾਂ ਅਤੇ ਹਿੰਸਾ ਦਾ ਦਿਖਾਵਾ ਕਦੇ ਵੀ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਦਾ ਭਵਿੱਖ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੋ ਸਕਦਾ।

* ਅੱਜ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਨੂੰ ਹਥਿਆਰਾਂ ਦੀ ਨਹੀਂ, ਸਗੋਂ ਸ਼ਾਂਤੀ, ਤਰੱਕੀ ਅਤੇ ਭਾਈਚਾਰਕ ਸਾਂਝ ਦੀ ਲੋੜ ਹੈ। 🕊️🌾

* ये 90 के दशक की तस्वीरें हैं, आज के किसी प्री-वेडिंग फोटोशूट की नहीं।
* AK-47 और ड्रम मैगजीन लेकर घूमने वाले ये कौन थे, जिनके सिरों पर लाखों का इनाम था?
* जिन्होंने निर्दोष लोगों को बसों और बाज़ारों से निकालकर गोलियों का निशाना बनाया।
* शाम के 5 बजते ही पूरे पंजाब में सन्नाटा पसर जाता था। माताओं के लाल, बहनों के भाई एक झटके में खत्म कर दिए जाते थे।
* आज भी उन परिवारों के बच्चे वो खौफनाक रातें याद करके कांप जाते हैं।
* यह था 90 का दशक—खून से लिखा इतिहास। चली हुई गोली ने कभी हिंदू या सिख नहीं देखा।
* हथियारों और हिंसा का दिखावा कभी भी पंजाब का भविष्य नहीं हो सकता।
* आज पंजाब को हथियारों की नहीं, बल्कि शांति, तरक्की और आपसी भाईचारे की जरूरत है।

* These are pictures from the 90s, not from some modern-day pre-wedding photoshoot.
* Who were these people roaming around with AK-47s and drum magazines, carrying bounties of lakhs on their heads?
* The ones who pulled innocent people out of buses and markets to make them targets of their bullets.
* As soon as the clock struck 5 PM, a dead silence would spread across Punjab. Sons of mothers and brothers of sisters were wiped out in an instant.
* Even today, the children of those families tremble at the memory of those terrifying nights.
* This was the 90s—a history written in blood. A fired bullet never differentiated between a Hindu or a Sikh.
* The display of weapons and violence can never be the future of Punjab.
* Today, Punjab does not need weapons; it needs peace, progress, and brotherhood.

tweets.title: Drishti-10 crashed today, and honestly, I'm not as concerned as many people seem to be. First and most importantly, there were no injuries or fatalities. These systems are still relatively new to our ecosystem, and training sorties exist for a reason. Every flight tests not just the drone, but the procedures, operators, maintenance chain, and the way the system performs in real conditions. Sometimes things go wrong, and when they do, valuable lessons are learned. India has a significant ISR gap compared to some of our adversaries. Platforms like Drishti-10 are part of the effort to close that gap. Whether a sortie is successful or ends in a crash, data is collected, shortcomings are identified, and the system improves. No military capability matures without setbacks. What matters is understanding why it happened and making sure the next flight is better than the last.
tweets.content:

Sometimes things go wrong, and when they do, valuable lessons are learned.

India has a significant ISR gap compared to some of our adversaries. Platforms like Drishti-10 are part of the effort to close that gap. Whether a sortie is successful or ends in a crash, data is collected, shortcomings are identified, and the system improves.

No military capability matures without setbacks. What matters is understanding why it happened and making sure the next flight is better than the last.
